text:
There once was a humble librarian named Clara. She believed in magic. She had read about it in an old book she discovered one sunny afternoon. Clara’s favorite magic happened sometimes—lights would flicker just in her fingertips.

She worked long hours shelving books. One night, after a particularly exhausting day, Clara curled up on a chair with her favorite novel. Just as sleep finally claimed her, a soft glow played around her. When she opened her eyes the next morning, everything was different. A gentle breeze was rustling the pages. She turned the page, and there it was: a spell to transform anything into a book you love.

In that glow, a figure spoke to her in a voice like old parchment. “You’re a seeker of wonder. Choose…”

She blinked to focus. The spell was intricate. She pictured a field of wildflowers. It turned into a garden. Then she wished for a book she had read many times that had disappeared. It materialized on the
